author: omerfirmak
content: Sounds good me to, I don’t make use of it anyway.

author: deme1744
content: Fine for me too. I too was using it to override the blob schedule that we already have from our static config per chain id. So no need for it.

author: jsign
content:
guys planning to merge this https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/3138
Please lmk if something doesn't sounds good.

(I can't merge since current `forks/amsterdam` is broken for amsterdam, so i cant properly rebase on top of `tests-glamsterdam-devnet@v7.1.0` common ancestor with `forks/amsterdam` correctly yet -- currently investigating with steel guys)

author: jsign
content:
extra note: now the two-bytes `SCHEMA_ID` has some meaningful semantics on each bytes, which means up to 256 forks, and up to 256 encodings per fork. Maybe the latter sounds a bit too much (prob this will be always 1 or up to 3).
BUt didn't want to introduce complexity reg non-8-bits boundaries stuff either.

author: jsign
content:
Could also make it 3 bytes, and first 2 being fork num -- so we have more than 256 supported forks (although sounds that should be enough too).

tl;dr: ended up with what the PR proposes. just sharing thinking process
